name: Wegman's Fish W/tomatoes, Olives and Capers
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------------FOR THE SAUCE-------------------------------
1 teaspoon Capers, drained
12 Pitted Kalamata olives, halved
12 Sun-dried Tomatoes, drained, cut up (olive oil packed)
1 cup (28 oz) Canned Tomatoes with Basil
1/3 cup Chopped Parsley
Salt and pepper
1. Sprinkle fish lightly with salt and pepper and coat with flour.
2. Preheat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
3. Add fish to the pan and cook for 1 1/2 minutes on each side. (A little longer, 2 - 2 1/2 minutes, for Roughy which is thicker and denser than the others.)
4. Transfer fish to paper towels to drain while preparing the sauce.
5. Add capers, olives, sun-dried tomatoes, and canned tomatoes to the frying pan.
6. Simmer until heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
7. Add fish back into the pan with the sauce; simmer for two more minutes.
8. Transfer to a platter and garnish with parsley.
Shirley and I had full servings of this at a demo at a local Wegman's Supermarket. It was superb. The demo guy agreed with Kathy Pitt's caution to be very careful about overcooking fish.
